valence.description: Core affect: pleasure-displeasure (-1 to +1). -1 = deeply unpleasant/disturbing, 0 = neutral, +1 = deeply pleasant/joyful

arousal.description: Core affect: calm-intense (-1 to +1). -1 = deeply calming/meditative, 0 = neutral, +1 = intensely activating/thrilling

dominance.description: Core affect: overwhelming-empowering (-1 to +1). -1 = viewer feels helpless/overwhelmed, 0 = neutral, +1 = viewer feels empowered/in-control

absorptionPotential.description: Zillmann's absorption: how cognitively consuming (0 to 1). 0 = background-watchable, 1 = demands total attention, monopolizes mental resources

hedonicValence.description: Experience type: fun/pleasure/escape/entertainment (0 to 1). How much does this film deliver pure enjoyment?

eudaimonicValence.description: Experience type: meaning/insight/being-moved/reflection (0 to 1). How much does this film provoke deep thought or emotional growth?

psychologicallyRichValence.description: Experience type: novelty/complexity/perspective-broadening (0 to 1). How much does this film challenge worldview or offer unusual perspectives?

emotionalArc.description: Reagan et al.'s six story shapes. rags-to-riches=steady rise, riches-to-rags=steady fall, man-in-a-hole=fall then rise, icarus=rise then fall, cinderella=rise-fall-rise, oedipus=fall-rise-fall

dominantEmotions.description: Top 2-3 emotions from Plutchik's wheel: joy, trust, fear, surprise, sadness, disgust, anger, anticipation

moodTags.description: Thematic mood tags for semantic affinity matching. Use lowercase single words or hyphenated phrases. e.g. redemption, isolation, coming-of-age, dark-humor

reasoning.description: Brief 1-2 sentence explanation of why you scored it this way
